meaning: n.<美>过分表示亲密友好的人
examples: He's a real backslapper if he thinks you're important, but if he finds out you're not a bigshot, he won't even bother to say hello to you.
Yet whereas Mr Cheney was seen as the grey eminence behind the sometimes buffoonish Mr Bush, Mr Biden seems a cheery backslapper compared to the professorial Mr Obama.
